intro: The most concrete split between Zipy and Maze is the pricing model: Zipy runs on subscription from $0/month, with a free tier, while Maze uses freemium. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Zipy carries medium lock-in (session replay data exportable) and subscription from $0/month, with a free tier; Maze carries medium lock-in (research data exportable) and freemium. Zipy is a fit when you are ux debugging and optimization. Maze is a fit when you are ux and design teams validating prototypes and usability pre-launch. The honest trade-off: Zipy is explicitly not for apps with strict privacy requirements (healthcare); Maze is explicitly not for engineering teams looking for production session replay and error triage. On the product side, Zipy is described as AI-powered session replay and error monitoring platform correlating frontend bugs with user session recordings, and Maze is described as User research and testing platform for rapid prototype testing, card sorting, tree testing, surveys and usability analytics with session recordings.
quick_take: Zipy is for ux debugging and optimization; Maze is for ux and design teams validating prototypes and usability pre-launch; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Zipy if its subscription pricing model fits your budget shape better than Maze's freemium model, and you are ux debugging and optimization.
verdict_b: Choose Maze if its freemium pricing model fits your budget shape better than Zipy's subscription model, and you are ux and design teams validating prototypes and usability pre-launch.

faq.a: Both tools use subscription pricing. Zipy is listed as subscription from $0/month, with a free tier; Maze as freemium. Without specific volume and seat assumptions it is not possible to say which is cheaper in general.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Zipy to Maze?
faq.a: Migration from Zipy to Maze is feasible but not trivial: Zipy has medium lock-in (session replay data exportable). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

On our scoring Zipy rates 4/5 for developer experience versus 3/5 for Maze. That suggests day-to-day workflows (CLI, SDKs, docs) are smoother in Zipy. Scores are directional; run a short spike on your own stack to confirm.

faq.q: Is Maze a good alternative to Zipy?
faq.a: Maze is pitched at ux and design teams validating prototypes and usability pre-launch, which overlaps with Zipy's fit (ux debugging and optimization) but is not identical. Note that Maze is explicitly not for engineering teams looking for production session replay and error triage, so it only replaces Zipy if you are outside that exclusion. Pricing models differ (subscription vs freemium), which may shift total cost.
